steps.text: Install the CEMS and prepare the RM test site according to the specifications in Section 3.
Prepare the CEMS for operation according to the manufacturer’s written instructions.
Determine the magnitude of the calibration drift (CD) and zero drift (ZD) once each day (at 24-hour intervals) for 7 consecutive days according to the procedure given in Section 6.
Conduct a RA test following the CD test period according to the procedure given in Section 7.

preconditions.text: Before determining the magnitude of the calibration drift (CD) and zero drift (ZD), ensure the affected facility is operating at more than 50 percent of normal load, or as specified in an applicable subpart.
Before conducting the RA test, ensure the affected facility is operating at more than 50 percent of normal load, or as specified in the applicable subpart.

constraints.text: All CD determinations must be made following a 24-hour period during which no unscheduled maintenance, repair, or manual adjustment of the CEMS took place.

steps.text: Conduct the CD test immediately before the adjustments, or conduct it in such a way that the CD and ZD can be determined.
Conduct the CD and ZD tests at the points specified in Sections 4.3 and 4.4.
Record the CEMS response and calculate the CD.
Conduct the RM measurements in such a way that they will yield results representative of the emissions from the source and can be correlated to the CEMS data.
Determine the RA at three levels (0 to 20, 40 to 60, and 80 to 120 percent of the emission limit) for one of the metals which will be monitored, or for iron.
Determine RA at three levels for one of the metals or for iron.
Correlate the CEMS and RM test data as to the time and duration.
Obtain a minimum of three pairs of CEMS and RM measurements for each metal required and at each level required.
Use Method 3B or its approved alternative as the reference method for diluent concentration.
Summarize the results on a data sheet.
Calculate the mean of the RM values.
Calculate the arithmetic differences between the RM and CEMS output sets.
Calculate the mean of the differences.
Calculate the standard deviation of each data set and CEMS RA.
Perform the RA test if metals emissions concentrations are undetectable.
